note: LIVE-data manifest for lean-6module-v1.1 freshness gate. Every LIVE field carries >=2 independent sources fetched at as_of; verify_freshness.py cross-checks these against an independent Yahoo re-fetch and the decision_card value. FILED data (Q2 FY2026 8-K / 10-Q line items) rides the dated-source claim_ledger discipline. CONTEXT: as_of 2026-06-22 is an OPEN trading session (market mid-session); the last print <= as_of from the Yahoo daily series is $1,184.88, which is what verify_freshness.py re-fetches, so as_of_price is set byte-identical to that. CRITICAL CALENDAR NOTE: MU fiscal Q3 2026 earnings drop 2026-06-24 (2 days AFTER as_of) -> the latest REPORTED quarter is Q2 FY2026 (ended 2026-02-26, reported 2026-03-18). Q3 is the imminent binding event, NOT yet a fact.

live_fields.low_high_hug_justification: Price IS legitimately ~1.6% off the 52-week high because MU is at/near an ALL-TIME HIGH on 2026-06-22 (the daily series climbs monotonically into as_of: 6/15 $1,088 -> 6/16 $1,021 -> 6/17 $1,043 -> 6/18 $1,134 -> 6/22 $1,185). This is the genuine last close, NOT a series-extreme grabbed by mistake (the INC-001 failure mode). The 52wk high ($1,204.50) was itself set within days of as_of. Independently confirmed: the live Yahoo re-fetch returns ~$1,188 and stockanalysis ~$1,182 for the same session -- all clustered near the high because the stock is making new highs, not because the wrong value was picked.
live_fields.note: Two INDEPENDENT same-session sources: Yahoo $1,184.88 (last print <= as_of, open session) and stockanalysis $1,181.82 (6/22 09:59 EDT intraday) -> delta 0.26% < 1% tol. as_of_price set to Yahoo's value for byte-identity with the gate's re-fetch (live re-fetch ~$1,188 is the still-moving intraday, ~0.3% off, within tol). NOTE: gurufocus $1,151.80 was the 6/21 PRIOR-day close -> a different session, so it is NOT used as a concurrent price source (would inject a ~2.9% session-to-session delta); it is retained only as corroboration of the price level/trend in the source_register.
Derived 1,130M sh x $1,184.88 = $1.339T vs stockanalysis $1.33T (0.67%). Shares ~1.13B per stockanalysis + ~1,123-1,130M diluted per filings.
Yahoo intraday 52wk high $1,204.50 (set 6/22 session) vs stockanalysis $1,196.61. Both reflect an all-time high set within days of as_of.
52wk low $103.38 identical across sources. Current price $1,184.88 is ~11.5x the 52wk low -> the stock has gained ~820-850% over the trailing year (a vertical AI-memory re-rating).

stockanalysis 1.13B; Q2 FY26 diluted weighted ~1,142M (GAAP NI $13,785M / $12.07 EPS = 1,142M). ~1.1% spread between basic/diluted-weighted; use ~1,130M for mcap identity. Buybacks ($650M H1 FY26) modestly reduce share count.

live_fields.value: Q3 FY2026 (ending ~2026-05) guidance issued 2026-03-18 with Q2 results: Revenue $33.5B +/- $750M (GAAP & non-GAAP), gross margin ~81% (GAAP & non-GAAP), operating expenses ~$1.60B GAAP / ~$1.40B non-GAAP, diluted EPS $18.90 +/- $0.40 GAAP / $19.15 +/- $0.40 non-GAAP. IMMINENT EVENT: actual Q3 FY2026 results report 2026-06-24 (2 days after as_of) -> this guide is the latest official forward number; the actual print is NOT yet available and is the single largest near-term catalyst/risk.

No new material adverse litigation/regulatory event identified at as_of. Background: China CAC 2023 ruling restricting MU products in certain critical-infrastructure procurement remains in effect (immaterial to current results given the AI-memory demand surge and sold-out HBM). CHIPS Act incentives ($2.26B government incentives netted against capex in H1 FY26) are a positive, not a liability. Export-control exposure is on the DEMAND side (US restrictions on AI chips to China affect end-customers) rather than a direct MU clawback. No active securities litigation flagged in recent disclosures.
